request.question: What was Debbie Rowe's husband dubbed?

title: Jackson v. Jackson
Lin Huiyin
Michael Jackson
Debbie Rowe
Debbie Purdy

Snarling Beasties
Gallery of Fear
List of The Millers episodes
Paris Jackson (actress)
sentences: Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson settled the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe.
Full custody was granted to Jackson.
Rowe is allowed visitation rights.
She gets property in Palm Beach, Van Nuys and Miami.
Rowe alleged sexual abuse as mentioned in the last amendment in the divorce.
Lin Huiyin (; known as Phyllis Lin or Lin Whei-yin when in the United States; 10 June 1904 – 1 April 1955) was a noted 20th-century Chinese architect and writer.

She is known to be the first female architect in modern China and her husband the famed "Father of Modern Chinese Architecture" Liang Sicheng, both of whom worked as founders and faculty in the newly formed Architecture Department of Northeastern University in 1928 and, after 1949, as professors in Tsinghua University in Beijing.
Liang and Lin began restoration work on cultural heritage sites of China in the post-imperial Republican Era of China; a passion which she would pursue to the end of her life.
The American artist Maya Lin is her niece.
<ref name = "Rowe/Kuan">Peter G. Rowe, Seng Kuan, "Architectural Encounters With Essence and Form in Modern China", MIT Press, 2002, p.219, ISBN  </ref>
Michael Joseph Jackson (August 29, 1958 – June 25, 2009) was an American singer, songwriter, and dancer.
Dubbed the "King of Pop", he was one of the most popular entertainers in the world, and was the best-selling music artist at the time of his death.
Jackson's contributions to music, dance, and fashion along with his publicized personal life made him a global figure in popular culture for over four decades.
Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) is an American nurse known for her marriage to Michael Jackson, with whom she had two children.
She lives in Palmdale, California.

Debbie Purdy (4 May 1963 – 23 December 2014) was a British music journalist and political activist from Bradford, West Yorkshire, with primary progressive multiple sclerosis, notable for her challenge to the law in England and Wales as relates to assisted suicide.
On 20 September 2009, it was announced that guidelines on assisted suicide law would be published by the UK Government.

The guidelines for England and Wales "come after a legal battle won by Debbie Purdy", as "Law Lords accepted earlier this year that [Purdy] had a right to know whether her husband would be prosecuted if he helped her to travel abroad to commit suicide."
Buy Me is a television program that has aired on HGTV in the U.S. since 2005, and on HGTV Canada since 2003, where it is that cable network's most popular show.
It is also seen in Belgium and South Africa, either dubbed or with subtitles.
It is produced by Whalley-Abbey Media Holdings (WHAM), which is owned by Debbie Travis and her husband, and produced "Debbie Travis’ Facelift".
The Snarling Beasties were formed in 1986 by Debbie Isitt and Mark Kilmurry and were influenced by the theatre of Steven Berkoff.
After performing "East" by Berkoff, Isitt became the theatre company's writer and director.
Mark Kilmurry played the lead parts in her first plays, "Gangsters" (1988), "Punch & Judy", (1989), "Femme Fatal" (1990) and "The Woman Who Cooked Her Husband" (1992).
The theatre company was very successful and won many awards including the Time Out theatre award, The Perrier Pick of the Fringe (three times) and The Independent Theatre Award.
Their theatre mixed a cinematic style with total theatre and created physical, dynamic and naturalistic plays.
The company was very influential but disbanded in 1996 though Debbie and Mark are both very successful in their chosen creative roles and still plan to do more work in the future.
Gallery of Fear (2013) is a four-part horror anthology, directed by Alan Rowe Kelly and Anthony G. Summer.
Kelly also wrote the screenplay for the film based on a story by Doug Smith.
The film stars Debbie Rochon, Raine Brown, and Susan Adriensen.
The four segments of the anthology are titled "Critics Choice," "By Her Hand, She Draws You Down," "Down the Drain," and "A Far Cry from Home."
The film was distributed by Southpaw Pictures and Tiny Core Pictures.
"The Millers" is an American sitcom that was created by Greg Garcia.
The multi-camera series premiered on CBS as part of the 2013–14 American television season and aired from October 3, 2013 to July 18, 2015.
"The Millers" is set in Leesburg, Virginia where Nathan Miller is a local television news reporter.
His sister Debbie runs a combination yoga studio/vegan restaurant with her husband Adam, with whom she has a daughter named Mikayla.
Nathan does not have any children and often hangs out with Ray, his friend who also serves as a cameraman for the fictional television station at which Nathan works.
As the series begins, Nathan and Debbie's bickering parents, Tom and Carol Miller, are returning from Myrtle Beach.
Nathan informs them he divorced his wife, Janice, several months ago.
Tom responds by deciding to leave Carol after being married for forty-three years.
Tom moves in with Debbie and Adam while Carol moves in with Nathan, each driving their children crazy.
On March 13, 2014, "The Millers" was renewed for a second season, which premiered on October 20, 2014.
Paris-Michael Katherine Jackson (born April 3, 1998) is an American actress, activist, and model.
She is the second child and only daughter of Michael Jackson and Debbie Rowe.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What was Debbie Rowe's husband dubbed?
hop1_docs: Buy Me | Buy Me is a television program that has aired on HGTV in the U.S. since 2005, and on HGTV Canada since 2003, where it is that cable network's most popular show.  It is also seen in Belgium and South Africa, either dubbed or with subtitles.  It is produced by Whalley-Abbey Media Holdings (WHAM), which is owned by Debbie Travis and her husband, and produced "Debbie Travis’ Facelift".

Debbie Burton | Debbie Burton was an American singer.  She is best known for dubbing the singing voice of the young Baby Jane Hudson (played by child actress Julie Allred) in the 1962 film "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane? ", singing the song "I've Written a Letter to Daddy".  Burton also sang a duet with Bette Davis, the rock and roll song "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?" , written by Frank DeVol and Lukas Heller.  It was released as a promotional single, with Burton's rendition of "I've Written a Letter to Daddy" on the flipside.  An instrumental version of "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?"  can be heard in the movie.

Betty Noyes | Elizabeth Noyes Hand (October 11, 1912 – December 24, 1987) was a singer and actress best known for dubbing two of Debbie Reynolds' numbers in the 1952 film "Singin' in the Rain."  Today, this is a well-known example of dubbing in a movie musical; ironically, Reynolds's character in "Singin' in the Rain" was supposedly dubbing for another character.

Jackson v. Jackson | Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson settled the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe.  Full custody was granted to Jackson.  Rowe is allowed visitation rights.  She gets property in Palm Beach, Van Nuys and Miami.  Rowe alleged sexual abuse as mentioned in the last amendment in the divorce.
Debbie Rowe | Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) is an American nurse known for her marriage to Michael Jackson, with whom she had two children.  She lives in Palmdale, California.

Debbie Boyd | Debbie Boyd (born November, 1957 in Gainesville, Florida) is a Representative in the Florida House of Representatives.  She received her associate degree in building construction from Santa Fe Community College in 1990.  She resides in Newberry, Florida and lives with her husband Tommy Boyd.

Debbi Fields | Debbi Fields (born September 18, 1956) is the founder and spokesperson of Mrs. Fields Bakeries.  Additionally, she has written several cookbooks and makes cookies.  She currently lives in Nashville, Tennessee, with her husband, former Holiday Inn and Harrah's CEO Michael Rose.
response.summary_1: Evidence: "Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) ... known for her marriage to Michael Jackson" and "Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson" show Debbie Rowe's husband is Michael Jackson. Missing fact/bridge: the passages do not state which epithet/nickname Michael Jackson was "dubbed." Next hop direction: retrieve Michael Jackson's biography or reliable sources (search for Michael Jackson + the word "dubbed" or "dubbed" plus "nickname"/"epithet") to find the specific title he was called.
response.hop2_query: Michael Jackson dubbed King of Pop nickname
hop2_docs: King of Pop | King of Pop is an honorific nickname most commonly associated with American pop icon Michael Jackson but may also refer to:

Michael Jackson | Michael Joseph Jackson (August 29, 1958 – June 25, 2009) was an American singer, songwriter, and dancer.  Dubbed the "King of Pop", he was one of the most popular entertainers in the world, and was the best-selling music artist at the time of his death.  Jackson's contributions to music, dance, and fashion along with his publicized personal life made him a global figure in popular culture for over four decades.

List of artists influenced by Michael Jackson | Michael Jackson, also known as the King of Pop, was a pop, music, dance and fashion icon.  His distinctive sound and style has influenced numerous artists of various music genres.

King of Pop (album) | King of Pop is a compilation album by American recording artist Michael Jackson released in commemoration of Michael Jackson's 50th birthday.  The album differs significantly in each country it was released because fan voting was employed to determine the songs for each country's version.  The album's title comes from the title Jackson acquired approximately 20 years earlier.  The album's launch was made public on June 20, 2008, with the official announcement of the Australian version.  The first release came with the German edition (which is identical to the Swiss edition) on August 22, 2008.

One More Chance (Michael Jackson song) | "One More Chance" is a song by American recording artist Michael Jackson.  It was penned by R. Kelly and co-produced by Jackson and Kelly for inclusion on Jackson's compilation album "Number Ones", released in 2003.  The song was later included on the Australian, French, Swedish and Indonesian editions of "King of Pop" and the Japanese limited edition of "The Ultimate Collection" box set.

Cultural impact of Michael Jackson | American entertainer Michael Jackson, who is known as the "King of Pop", is considered one of the most successful and influential entertainers of all time.  He had an unparalleled influence all over the world through his musical, dance, fashion and humanitarian contributions.

Unity: The Latin Tribute to Michael Jackson | Unity: The Latin Tribute to Michael Jackson, also known as The Unity Project, is a collaborative musical project aimed at creating a Michael Jackson tribute album inspired by Latin music styles.  The leader of the project is Peruvian-American musician Tony Succar and vocals are provided by a number of leading artists in Latin music genres, especially Salsa.  Succar's aim with the project was to celebrate Jackson's music and legacy by combining it with a Latin-orchestrated sound.  The stated goal is "to honor the legacy of Michael Jackson, the King of Pop.  By remaining true to his artistic essence, excellence, and music we seek to unify all human beings."  Upon its release, the album debuted at number one on the "Billboard" Tropical Albums chart.
hop2_titles: List of artists influenced by Michael Jackson
Unity: The Latin Tribute to Michael Jackson
response.summary_2: Debbie Rowe's husband was Michael Jackson. Michael Joseph Jackson was widely "dubbed the 'King of Pop'" (passage [2]).
